summary: Intense cross‑border hostilities continue between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, with frequent Israeli airstrikes on Hezbollah infrastructure and retaliatory rocket and drone attacks. Israeli forces report killing dozens of Hezbollah operatives and damaging launch sites, while Hezbollah’s drone and rocket attacks have caused limited Israeli injuries. Parallel political turbulence in Israel includes coalition disputes over legislation and a pending Netanyahu security meeting.
key_points: Israeli airstrikes hit over 40 Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon, killing ~15 operatives and destroying weapon depots and rocket launchers.
Hezbollah launched multiple rockets and drone attacks on Israeli border communities, causing a few injuries and triggering sirens.
Israeli reports of strikes on specific Lebanese locations (Burg al‑Shamali, Yanoch, and the town of Sour) following evacuation warnings.
Israeli domestic politics: coalition debates over investigation committee, senior civil service dismissal law, and opposition stance on draft exemption law.
No major territorial shifts reported; front lines remain along the Israel‑Lebanon border.
casualties_mentioned: Approximately 15 Hezbollah operatives killed; a non‑commissioned officer moderately wounded and a reservist lightly injured on the Israeli side; separate civilian fatality in Haifa unrelated to the conflict.
territorial: No significant changes reported; engagements remain confined to border areas and targeted infrastructure.
